testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. All test failures are due to implementation gaps.

criteria.criterion: AC-1: runSemanticReview() signature includes featureName parameter
AC-2: runReview() in runner.ts includes featureName and forwards it
AC-3: ReviewOrchestrator.review() includes featureName and forwards it
AC-4: review.ts stage passes ctx.prd.feature as featureName
AC-5: buildSessionName() deterministic
AC-6: runSemanticReview() signature contains featureName parameter
AC-7: runReview() has featureName param and passes it to runSemanticReview
AC-8: ReviewOrchestrator.review() has featureName parameter
AC-9: review.ts passes ctx.prd.feature as featureName
AC-10: semantic.ts uses buildSessionName() instead of hardcoded string
AC-11: createDebateSession() receives featureName (not story.id) - debate path
AC-12: undefined-featureName code path calls buildSessionName() with debug log
AC-13: No occurrences of nax-semantic-${story.id} template literal
criteria.note: Test expects grep to fail (no matches), but grep found matches in non-src paths or in debug output

criteria.criterion: AC-14: Non-debate path calls agent.run() NOT agent.complete()
criteria.note: Implementation uses agent.complete() at line 480, should use agent.run()

criteria.criterion: AC-15: agent.run() acpSessionName uses buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'implementer')
criteria.note: Session name uses 'semantic' role instead of 'implementer'; also uses complete() not run()

criteria.criterion: AC-16: agent.run() keepSessionOpen === false in non-debate path
criteria.note: Using complete() which doesn't set keepSessionOpen; should use run()

criteria.criterion: AC-17: runSemanticReview signature includes featureName and buildSessionName called correctly
AC-18: Logger.debug called with 'implementer session not found' when session is new
criteria.note: Expected message not found in semantic.ts; should be logged when session.created === false

criteria.criterion: AC-19: rawResponse from AgentRunResult.output (not CompleteResult)
AC-20: runSemanticReview() return value satisfies ReviewCheckResult
criteria.note: Incomplete due to implementation using complete() instead of run()

criteria.criterion: AC-21: Debate path invokes agent.complete() when debate enabled
AC-22-29: resolveOutcome() workdir/featureName parameters
criteria.note: resolveOutcome() called at session-stateful.ts:269 without passing workdir/featureName

issues.issue: Non-debate semantic review path uses agent.complete() instead of agent.run()
issues.details: The code should call agent.run() with proper session management for non-debate path, but instead calls agent.complete(). This affects AC-14, AC-15, AC-16, AC-18, AC-19.
issues.impact: 11 acceptance tests failing due to this single architectural flaw

issues.issue: Session role is 'semantic' but should be 'implementer'
issues.details: buildSessionName() is called with 'semantic' as the role, but it should be 'implementer' per AC-15. Also note this line is in the wrong code path (complete vs run).
issues.impact: Breaks session continuity design; prevents implementer session resumption

issues.issue: Missing log message when implementer session not found
issues.details: Expected to log 'implementer session not found — semantic review running in new session' when agent.run() returns sessionCreated===false, but this logic is missing entirely since code uses complete() instead.

issues.issue: resolveOutcome() not receiving workdir and featureName parameters
issues.details: Call to resolveOutcome() has 6 parameters but should have 8: workdir (line 269 after ctx.timeoutSeconds) and featureName should be added to enable session resumption in synthesis/judge resolvers.
issues.impact: Debate resolution cannot resume implementer session for semantic review stage

issues.details: buildSessionName() expects role to match the session role wired in AgentRunOptions, but 'semantic' doesn't match session lifecycle expectations.

reasoning: The implementation has correctly added the featureName parameter through the call chain (review.ts → orchestrator.ts → runner.ts → semantic.ts) and it's being passed correctly from the stage. However, the non-debate path implementation in semantic.ts has a critical architectural flaw: it uses agent.complete() instead of agent.run(). This breaks 11 acceptance criteria. Additionally, resolveOutcome() in the debate path is not receiving the workdir and featureName parameters needed for session resumption. The session role is also incorrectly set to 'semantic' instead of 'implementer'. These are not minor issues that can be fixed during verification—they require architectural changes to the implementation. The test writer correctly anticipated these requirements, but the implementer chose a different approach that doesn't meet the story's acceptance criteria. Cannot approve until these critical issues are resolved.
